text: and ready-to-eat foods can be prepared for use as complementary foods (Refer page 33). Such foods should be stored in clean bottles or tins. As feeding is likely to be time consuming, the cup or the plate from which the recipe is being fed to the infant should be kept covered to protect it from flies. Most often, diarrhea is caused by unhygienic practices adopted by mothers. The weaning foods which are properly cleaned and well-cooked are safe even for young infants. /c63Breast-milk alone is not enough for infants after 6 months of age. /c63Complementary foods should be given after 6 months of age, in addition to breast-feeding. /c63Do not delay complementary feeding. /c63Feed low-cost home-made complementary foods. /c63Feed complementary food on demand 3-4 times a day. /c63Provide fruits
